title: Scope and Purpose
section:
Scope and Purpose
Use this page to understand what Back Office covers, who uses it, and how this documentation should be consumed.

Overview
Playage Back Office is the central operational console for day-to-day platform administration. It brings operations, finance, support, reporting, and configuration workflows into one controlled environment.

Purpose
The Back Office exists to keep operations:
Reliable in real-time execution
Traceable through audit-ready workflows
Governed by role-based permissions
Scalable across teams and regions

Scope
This documentation covers the core BO domains:
Getting started and secure access
Player and user operations
Reporting and analytics modules
Financial reporting and reconciliation support
Operations/support handling workflows
System and admin-level configuration controls

Intended Audience
BO administrators and team leads
Operations and support teams
Finance and reconciliation teams
Compliance and risk reviewers
Product and configuration owners

How to Use This Guide
Start from the BO home section and choose your functional area.
Follow pages in the recommended order inside each section.
Use filter and validation checklists before exporting critical reports.
Apply troubleshooting steps before escalating.

Success Criteria
A reader should be able to:
Perform daily BO tasks with minimal confusion
Validate outputs before decision making
Escalate only when required, with complete context
Keep workflows consistent across teams

Best Practice
Treat this documentation as an operational runbook: update it whenever process or policy changes happen.

title: Getting Started
section:
Getting Started
Use this section to complete the first Back Office access flow with the correct sequence and minimum risk.

Scope
This guide covers:
Login and authentication checks
Platform selection after login
First dashboard validation
Initial access verification for operations

Before You Start
Confirm you are using the correct environment URL (staging or production).
Ensure your account is active and assigned to the correct role.
Keep 2FA device/app ready if your account requires OTP.
Avoid shared browsers for admin access.

Recommended Reading Order
Login and Authentication
Administration Dashboard
Admin Back Office Dashboard

Expected Result
After completing this section, you should be able to:
Sign in successfully
Open Back Office from the platform selector
Validate key dashboard widgets and menu visibility
Confirm your role-based access is correct

Common Issues During Onboarding
Login URL opens wrong environment : verify domain and saved bookmarks.
Backoffice card is missing : role permission or module mapping is incomplete.
Partial menu visibility : account policy changed and requires re-login.

Best Practice
Keep this section as a standard onboarding checklist for every new admin.
Re-run this checklist after role updates, policy changes, or environment switch.

title: Login and Authentication
section:
Login and Authentication
Use this flow to sign in securely and verify your Back Office session is valid.

When to Use
First login of the day
New user onboarding
Access verification after password or role changes

Video Walkthrough (Login + Logout)
Your browser does not support the video tag.
Reference video for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/video/login-logout-bo.mp4

Login Flow (Step by Step)
Open the official Back Office URL.
Verify domain + https:// .
Enter registered email.
Enter password.
Click Sign In .
Complete OTP/2FA if prompted.
Wait for redirect to dashboard or module selector.
Confirm role-based modules are visible in the left navigation.
Verify the top-right profile belongs to the expected account.

Logout Steps
Open the profile/account menu from the top-right.
Click Logout / Sign Out .
Confirm you are redirected to the login screen.
Refresh a protected BO URL to verify the session is closed.
Confirm browser back navigation does not reopen protected pages.

Field Notes
Email : Must match the admin account.
Password : Case-sensitive.
Forgot Password : Use reset flow if enabled.

Expected Result
Account opens without errors.
Correct modules are visible for your role.
Session remains stable (no instant logout).
Protected routes require fresh authentication after logout.

Troubleshooting
Invalid credentials : Check keyboard layout, caps, and email typo.
2FA failure : Sync device time and generate a fresh OTP.
Account locked : Request unlock from system admin.
Redirect loop : Clear site cookies and sign in again.

Security Tips
Never share password/OTP.
Avoid shared or unmanaged devices.
Always sign out after sensitive operations.

title: Player Management and Analytics
section:
Player Management and Analytics
This page is used for both player onboarding (Add Player) and ongoing player list monitoring.

Add Player Video Walkthrough
Your browser does not support the video tag.
Reference video for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/video/add-player.mp4

Add Player Workflow
Open Add Player from the Player Management page.
Fill required fields: username, email, phone (if required), country/currency, and account status.
Confirm security/verification defaults (password policy, verification state, initial KYC state).
Assign role/segment/tag if your operation flow requires it.
Save the form and verify the success confirmation.
Search the new player in the list.
Open player details and validate profile data.

Add Player Fields: Required vs Optional
Based on * markers shown in the video flow:

Required Fields
Username
Password
First Name
Last Name
Gender
Email
Mobile
Currency
Region
Document ID

Optional Fields
Middle Name
Address
Zip Code
City
Language
Phone
BirthDate
Is Resident (checkbox)
Subscribed To Email (checkbox)

Account Status Field
Account provides Open and Blocked options.
Use Open for active onboarding, Blocked to keep account access restricted.
Near submit flow, if the field shows Select Status , pick the proper status before completing creation.

Document ID Rule (Critical)
Document ID must contain exactly 11 digits .
Video validation message: Document / ID should contain exactly 11 digits .
Alphanumeric values or wrong length trigger validation errors.
Examples:
Valid: 55776688541
Invalid: 8885HU8859 (contains letters)
Invalid: 557766885 (less than 11 digits)

Is Resident and Subscribed To Email
Both checkboxes can be turned on/off directly in Add Player.
Is Resident : sets resident status at creation time.
Subscribed To Email : sets initial email communication subscription.
Both are optional and depend on your onboarding policy.

Add Player Field Checklist
Username : must be unique.
Email : valid format and no duplicate.
Phone : should follow country format.
Country/Currency : must match operational rules.
Status : usually Active or Pending based on process.
Tag/Note : follow internal naming conventions for tracking.

Add Player Expected Result
Player is created without validation errors.
New record appears in player list.
Player detail page shows consistent base profile values.
Record is ready for next KYC/communication steps.

Player List Monitoring Workflow
Open Player Management dashboard.
Apply required filters (date, segment, status).
Click Refresh to load latest records.
Review player table and activity indicators.

Refresh and Data Loading
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-1/xuqwloklrrrtrwng-image-4b1aa2b4.webp
If table is empty on first load, use Refresh .
Refresh pulls updated data from API/server.
Re-run refresh after changing filter criteria.

Player Table Review
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/bo/player-management/3-1-1/qv8ymt4fubthm5yu-image-eeed3c14.webp
Typical fields include:
Player ID and username
Verification state
Contact and profile data
Login/activity markers
Balance indicators

Troubleshooting
New player not visible after create : clear filters and search by username/email.
Duplicate conflict on create : validate whether username/email already exists.
Save error in Add Player form : re-check required fields and value formats.
No data after refresh : Clear filters and retry with wider date range.
Slow table loading : Reduce date span and use narrower filters.
Unexpected player values : Verify active currency/filter settings.

Best Practice
Filter first, then refresh for large datasets.
Review anomalies (login spikes, status changes) daily.
Keep access audits aligned with compliance requirements.

Step 1: Email Verify

Click Next .

Step 2: User Profile
Fill Username .
Fill Password .
Fill First Name and Last Name .
Select account status (default shown as Open ).
Fill Address .
Fill Phone .
Optional: enable Must Change Password .
Click Save .

Required vs Optional Fields
Based on the video form ( * marker):

Required fields
Email Verify email (step 1)
Username
Password
First Name
Last Name
Address
Phone

Optional fields
Account status change from default (if policy allows default Open )
Must Change Password checkbox

Field Behavior Notes
Email Verify is the onboarding gate before profile entry.
Must Change Password can be toggled during creation.
Back returns to previous step without saving user.
Save remains unavailable/blocked until required fields are valid.

Manage Existing User (After Creation)
Your browser does not support the video tag.
Reference video for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/video/edit-user.mp4
Open user details from row actions in the list.
Use the edit icon in User Info for profile updates.
Use Permissions tab for role and module access.
Use Password card edit icon for password reset/change.
Use Notes tab to add internal comments.
Use Logins tab to audit recent access.

Profile Modification
Update these fields from User Info edit mode:
Status ( Open /blocked state as per policy)
First Name and Last Name
Email
Address
Phone
Must Change Password
Email Authentication
Two Factor Authentication
Save with the check icon, or cancel with the close icon.

Access Role and Permissions
From the Permissions tab:
Click edit mode.
Search module/permission by keyword.
Move items between available and granted lists using arrows.
Expand module groups and select required actions.
Save changes.
Use Add Role if a new reusable role is required.

Password Change / Reset
From the password section:
Click edit icon.
Enter Password .
Enter Confirm Password .
Save.
Enable Must Change Password in profile when first-login reset is required.

Add Notes
From Notes tab:
Click New Note .
Enter comment in the note text area.
Click Save .
Saved notes appear in the notes table with date, username, and comment.

Login Audit
Open Logins tab to verify:
login date/time
source/session information (if enabled)
recent access pattern for troubleshooting

Troubleshooting
Cannot move to profile step : Verify email format in Email Verify .
Save button does not proceed : Check required profile fields ( Username , Password , First Name , Last Name , Address , Phone ).
User created but cannot sign in : Validate account status and password policy.
User not visible in table : Clear filters and search by exact username/email.
Save button does not create user : Check required fields and duplicate username.
User cannot access expected module : Recheck role assignment and permission toggles.
Password update fails : Ensure Password and Confirm Password match.
Permission update not applied : Re-login the target user and verify saved state.
Notes not visible : Confirm note was saved and filter/sort is not hiding newest row.

Best Practice
Apply least-privilege role design.
Keep 2FA enabled for high-privilege users.
Review inactive users regularly.

Click Add Role .
Enter role name.
Enable permissions by module.
Save role.

Edit or Delete Role
Edit : update module permissions or role scope.
Delete : allowed only when no users are assigned.

Recommended Workflow
Create role first.
Test role on one user account.
Confirm module visibility.
Roll out to remaining users.

Troubleshooting
Role not assignable in user form : Refresh page after role creation.
Permission change not reflected : Re-login affected user session.
Delete role blocked : Remove linked users first.

Best Practice
Keep roles business-function based (Support, Finance, Compliance).
Avoid direct ad-hoc permission changes without role review.

Repeated failures on one method : check provider incident panel.
Amount mismatch : validate fees/conversion rules.

Maintain a pending-age bucket (0-15m, 15-60m, 60m+) and resolve oldest first.

Wrong queue assignment : re-route to correct team queue.
Missing transaction context : request reference ID before action.

Invalid OTP attempts are blocked.
Backup/recovery path works for authorized user.

Troubleshooting
OTP rejected repeatedly : sync device time and request new code.
No 2FA prompt : verify enforcement policy for current role.
Device lost : trigger recovery flow via authorized admin process.

Best Practice
Require 2FA for all admin roles and audit exceptions monthly.

Minor UI spacing/visibility tweaks

Disable Custom CSS (Rollback)

title: Banners Overview
section:
Banners Overview
This guide follows the add-edit-page-top-banner flow for managing page top banners in CMS.

Video Walkthrough (Add/Edit Page Top Banner)
Your browser does not support the video tag.
Reference video for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/video/add-edit-page-top-banner.mp4

End-to-End SOP Flow
Open banner group by page.
Add top banner.
Edit/modify banner details.
Publish/Unpublish/Trash/Restore banner.
Adjust sequence number when needed.

Step 1: Open the Target Page Banner Group
Go to Blocks > Banners .
Use page rows (for example: Games, Sports, Home).
Open the target page row to enter its banner list.
Top actions visible in flow:
Add Banner Group (main page)
Add Banner (inside selected page group)
Edit Sequence No. (inside selected page group)

Step 2: Add a New Top Banner
Inside target page group, click Add Banner .
Fill General Details .
Fill Banner Properties .
Add optional editor content.
Upload media.
Click Save .

Step 3: Fill General Details
Fields shown in video:
Platform : multi-select ( Web , Mobile )
Status : Published , Unpublished , Trash
Show For Users : Guest , Logged In , Guest and Logged In
New Window : Yes or No
Sequence No. : ordering index
Notes:
Set status intentionally during create.
Sequence number controls display order among banners of the same page.

Step 4: Fill Banner Properties
Fields shown in flow:
Title *
Language ( Turkish / English )
Url type dropdown + URL input
Alt text
Editor (content block)
Media * upload (drag-drop or browse)
Validation behavior seen:
Missing required title shows validation.
Media * is mandatory for banner save.
Language rule:
If only English banner is needed, set language as English .
If only Turkish banner is needed, set language as Turkish .
For bilingual delivery, create separate records per language.

Step 5: Edit/Modify Existing Banner
Use row Edit action in page banner list.
Update details (status, audience, language, URL, media, sequence).
Save.

Step 6: Publish, Unpublish, Trash, Restore
Use row actions in selected page group:
Publish to make banner live.
Unpublish to move to draft state.
Trash to move item to recycle state.
Restore from Trash tab using available action.
Video confirmations include messages like:
Published! The item has been published.
Trash confirmation prompt (example: Are you sure you want to trash this item? ).

Step 7: Sequence Management
Use Sequence No. field in row or form.
Or open Edit Sequence No. for controlled ordering.
Save sequence updates.
Best practice:
Keep unique sequence numbers per page group.
Re-verify order after adding a new banner.

Filter and Verification Workflow
Use filters in page group list:
After each save/publish:
Check correct status tab ( Published , Unpublished , Trash ).
Check language and platform filters.
Confirm final banner appears in intended page zone.

Quick Troubleshooting
Banner not visible on site: verify Status , Platform , Show For Users , and page group.
Wrong language shown: filter by language and update/create language-specific record.
Save blocked: check required Title * and Media * .
Wrong order: correct Sequence No. and save again.
Trashed by mistake: restore from Trash tab.

title: Google reCAPTCHA Overview
section:
Google reCAPTCHA Overview
The Security > Google reCAPTCHA module is used to enable or disable reCAPTCHA protection across the website.
When enabled with valid keys, reCAPTCHA security applies site-wide on supported flows.

Interface Overview
Google reCAPTCHA settings page contains:
reCAPTCHA Protection toggle (Enable/Disable)
API keys section: Site Key (Public Key) Secret Key (Private Key)
Site Key (Public Key)
Secret Key (Private Key)
Save Configuration button
Setup instructions panel with key generation flow
Link to Google reCAPTCHA Admin Console
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/google-recaptcha/google-recaptcha-config-overview.webp

Configure and Apply reCAPTCHA
Open Security > Google reCAPTCHA .
Turn protection toggle ON .
Enter: Site Key (public) Secret Key (private)
Site Key (public)
Secret Key (private)
Click Save Configuration .
After saving valid keys, reCAPTCHA security is applied across configured website forms/pages.
To disable protection, turn toggle OFF and save configuration.

How to Generate Site Key and Secret Key
Use Google reCAPTCHA Admin Console:
Admin Console: https://www.google.com/recaptcha/admin
Recommended setup flow:
Open reCAPTCHA Admin Console.
Create a new site (or choose existing site).
Select reCAPTCHA v2 (as required by your panel flow).
Add your site domain (and CMS panel domain if required).
Generate keys.
Copy Site Key and Secret Key into CMS fields.
Enable and save configuration.

Best Practices
Keep Secret Key private and never expose it on frontend/public pages.
Use correct domain mapping in Google console to avoid verification failure.
Verify reCAPTCHA behavior after enabling (login/register/contact or relevant flows).
If issues occur, disable toggle temporarily and recheck key/domain setup.

Notes
Site Key is used in frontend integration.
Secret Key is used server-side to validate reCAPTCHA responses.

https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/telegram-setting/telegram-bot-settings/telegram-settings-overview-admin-tab.webp
Required fields:

Edit Flow
Click the edit icon on the target bot row/card.

Click Submit to save updates.
Admin modal examples:

https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/telegram-setting/telegram-bot-settings/telegram-settings-service-type-admin.webp

Enable / Disable Flow
Each bot card includes an Active toggle:
ON : bot is active and notifications are sent
OFF : bot is disabled and notifications stop
After toggle change, the system uses the current status for that service type.

Group Notification Setup Logic
For notifications to arrive in Telegram group:
Create bot using @BotFather .
Copy bot token.
Add bot to the target Telegram group.
Ensure bot has permission to send messages if required.
Add target members to that group.
Get the group chat id and set it in Group Id .
Once configured, event-based notifications are delivered through that bot to the group.

Where to Get Values
Bot Username and Bot Token : from @BotFather
Group Id : from Telegram Bot API ( getUpdates ) or helper chat-id bots after adding bot to group
Service Type : selected based on your notification use case

Operational Best Practices
Use separate bots for User and Admin channels.
Keep critical service types ( Withdrawal , KYC , System Report ) actively monitored.
If token is rotated, immediately update bot configuration.
Never expose tokens in public docs, screenshots, or logs.

title: Pages Overview
section:
Pages Overview
The Pages section is used to create and manage CMS content pages. Based on the current flow, this guide covers both Page Add and Category Add from Manage categories .

Video Walkthrough (Page Add + Category Add)
Your browser does not support the video tag.
Reference video for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/video/cms-page-add.mp4

What You Manage Here
Page records under Published , Unpublished , Trash
Language and category based filtering
New page creation from Add
Category creation from Manage categories

Required Order (Must Follow)
Create categories first (language-wise).
Create/design pages after category setup.
Map each page to matching language category.
If category is not created first, correct page mapping will fail or become inconsistent.

Step 1: Create Categories Language-wise (First)
Open Pages .
Click Manage categories .
Select Language = Turkish .
Click + Add Category and create Turkish category name.
Save with check icon.
Switch Language = English .
Click + Add Category and create English category name.
Save and close modal.
Important behavior from the current flow:
Category name must be unique for selected language.
Duplicate entry shows: Category name must be unique.
Turkish and English are separate language zones.
Same content group should exist in both languages as separate category records.
New categories become available in page form Category dropdown for their respective language.
Example:
Turkish category: test_demo_turk1
English category: test_demo_engl

Step 2: Create Turkish Page Content
Click Add .
Set Language = Turkish .
Select Turkish Category (created in Step 1).
Enter custom Turkish page Title .
Set Type and complete content: Type = Page -> fill Editor * Type = Link -> fill Link *
Click Save .

Step 3: Create English Page Content
Click Add .
Set Language = English .
Select English Category (created in Step 1).
Enter custom English page Title .
Set Type and complete content: Type = Page -> fill Editor * Type = Link -> fill Link *
Click Save .
Confirm success toast and verify records with language filter.

Step 4: Field Rules for Page Design
Field rules:
Title * and Category * are required.
Link * is required when Type = Link .
Editor * is required when Type = Page .
Validation shown in flow: Enter a valid relative path starting with / .
New Window is used with link behavior ( Yes / No ).

Step 5: Edit Existing Page
Locate record using Search , Language , Category filters.
Open row action Edit .
Update page details or content.
Save and re-check the row.

Step 6: Status and Lifecycle Operations
Use row actions based on page state:
Published : edit, unpublish, delete
Unpublished : publish, edit, delete
Trash : restore/publish path, permanent delete

Quick Troubleshooting
Category not visible in dropdown: add it from Manage categories using same language context.
Save not working in Type = Page : check Editor * is filled.
Save not working in Type = Link : check Link * and relative path format.
Duplicate category not saving: rename category to a unique value for that language.
Turkish page mapped to English category (or reverse): re-edit page and select matching language category.

Safe Operating Checklist
Create both Turkish and English categories first.
Create Turkish page with Turkish category.
Create English page with English category.
Use custom page name/title for each language.
Confirm language-category mapping before save.
Use Unpublished for review if needed.
Publish only after content QA.
Prefer trash/restore flow before permanent delete.

Flow 1: Edit Provider Image
Use this when provider branding image needs to be added or updated.
Click row Edit .
In provider edit form, upload/replace provider image.
Save changes.
Editable area in this flow:
Provider image upload section
(Provider name/type shown as reference and may be non-editable depending on role/config)
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/blocks/providers/providers-edit-form-image-upload.webp

Delete Existing Provider Image
From row actions, image delete option is available for removing current provider image.
After deletion, you can upload a new image from edit form.

Flow 2: Reorder Providers (Edit Order)
Use Edit Order to rearrange provider display priority.
Click Edit Order from top-right.
Update numeric order values per provider row.
Click Save to apply sequence.
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/blocks/providers/providers-edit-order-mode.webp

Recommended Operations Flow
Validate provider list using search/type filters.
Update provider image where needed.
Remove broken/old image and replace via edit form.
Use Edit Order to control frontend listing sequence.
Save and verify ordering/image reflection on target pages.

Notes
Provider creation/deletion should be handled from super-admin/back-office master configuration.
Keep image format/size consistent to maintain clean frontend appearance.

title: SEO Pages Overview
section:
SEO Pages Overview
The SEO > Pages module is used to manage custom SEO per page route .
You can:
Select an existing page and set custom SEO data
Add a page that does not yet have custom SEO
Update previously saved custom SEO
Delete custom SEO for a page
When custom SEO is deleted, that page falls back to global/default SEO configuration.

Interface Overview
Pages SEO screen includes:
Left panel: list of pages with custom SEO entries
Add Page button (for pages without custom SEO)
Right panel: SEO form fields + social media image
Language dropdown (English / Turkish)
Save / Cancel actions
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/seo/pages/seo-pages-overview-language-switch.webp

Add Custom SEO for a Page
Use this flow when a page does not yet have custom SEO.
Click Add Page .
In Select Page modal, choose page route/name.
Click Save in modal.
Fill SEO form fields for selected page.
Save form.
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/seo/pages/seo-pages-add-page-modal.webp
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/seo/pages/seo-pages-custom-entry-empty-form.webp
Typical fields in custom SEO form:
Title
Meta Description
Meta Keywords
Domain
Copyright
Facebook Id
Twitter Card
Facebook Article Author
Social Media Image

Language-wise Custom SEO
Language dropdown allows separate SEO entries per language (for example: Turkish and English ).
Behavior:
You can keep different metadata for each language.
Route/page renders SEO based on language context.
Same page can have different title/description/image for EN and TR.

Update Existing Custom SEO
To update a previously configured page SEO:
Select page from left panel.
Modify fields.
Replace or keep social media image.
Click Save .
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/seo/pages/seo-pages-custom-entry-filled-form.webp

Delete Custom SEO and Fallback Rule
You can delete a page-specific custom SEO entry from page list.
After deletion:
Custom SEO for that page is removed.
Page will use global/default SEO values instead of custom values.
This makes rollback simple when page-level override is no longer required.

Recommended Operations Flow
Define global SEO baseline first.
Add custom SEO only for priority landing pages.
Maintain EN/TR metadata separately where content differs.
Review image and meta fields before save.
Delete custom SEO only when global fallback is desired.

Notes
Keep meta titles/descriptions concise and relevant to page intent.
Ensure social image matches page language/content for better sharing previews.

title: Robots.txt Overview
section:
Robots.txt Overview
The SEO > Robots.txt section is used to edit crawler access rules for your site.
From this screen, you can:
Update robots.txt directives in editor
Save draft changes
Publish robots.txt changes to site

Interface Overview
The Robots.txt page contains:
Text editor for robots directives ( User-agent , Allow , Disallow , etc.)
Save button
Publish button
Info note about cache reflection window
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/seo/robots/robots-txt-editor-save-publish.webp

Update and Publish Flow
Open SEO > Robots.txt .
Edit directives in text area.
Click Save to store changes.
Click Publish to apply updated robots file.
Use this carefully, especially for Disallow rules, to avoid blocking important pages from crawlers.

Cache Reflection Note
Based on your panel note, published robots updates are reflected on site after cache window (for example around 72 hours ).
This means:
Save/Publish is immediate in CMS
Crawl-side reflection can be delayed by cache/CDN lifecycle

Recommended Safe Workflow
Validate syntax before publishing.
Keep a backup of previous robots directives.
Avoid broad Disallow: / unless intentional.
Publish during controlled deployment window.
Re-check robots response after cache expiry window.

Notes
Incorrect robots rules can impact SEO indexability and traffic.
Prefer minimal and targeted disallow rules.

title: Logo Web/Mobile Overview
section:
Logo Web/Mobile Overview
The Logo > Web/Mobile section is used to manage branding assets shown on website and mobile web surfaces.

Interface Overview
Web/Mobile logo panel contains multiple upload slots, including:
Header Logo
Mobile Logo
Footer Logo
Loader
Favicon
Each slot supports file upload/replace, and existing assets can be removed where delete action is available.
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/logo/web-mobile/web/logo-web-mobile-overview.webp

Supported Actions
For each logo block (based on availability in UI):
Upload new image
Replace existing image
Delete existing image
Save all updates
Common controls:
Upload icon (replace/upload)
Delete icon (remove current asset)
Save button (top-right)

Recommended Update Flow
Open Logo > Web/Mobile .
Update required slots (header/mobile/footer/loader/favicon).
Remove old asset if needed, then upload replacement.
Click Save .
Verify branding visibility on website and mobile views.

Notes
Keep file dimensions and formats aligned with slot requirements shown in UI.
Update favicon carefully, as it impacts browser tab branding.

title: Mail Providers
section:
Mail Providers
The Security > Mail Providers screen is used to manage the email delivery infrastructure used by the system.
This module supports two provider types:
API SMTP : third-party service based delivery
Mail SMTP : classic SMTP server based delivery

Multi-Provider and Fallback Logic
If the API SMTP provider becomes temporarily unavailable, email delivery should not stop. For this reason, multiple providers are configured with a defined priority order.
Recommended setup:
Primary: API SMTP
Backup: Mail SMTP

Interface Overview
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/mail-providers/mail-providers/mail-provider-overview.webp

Add a New Provider
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/mail-providers/mail-providers/mail-provider-add-form.webp

Priority and Fallback Flow
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/mail-providers/mail-providers/mail-provider-priority-fallback.webp

Operational Flow
Open Security > Mail Providers .
Click Add to create a new provider.
Select provider type ( API SMTP or Mail SMTP ).
Enter required host/auth/port and credential details.
Save the provider.
Configure provider priority (primary -> fallback).
Send a test email to verify both providers work correctly.

Notes
API-based providers can face rate limits or short outages; fallback should always be configured.
Keep SMTP credentials secure and never expose them in logs.
In production, keep at least one tested backup provider active.

title: Firebase Settings
section:
Firebase Settings
Use Security > Firebase Settings to manage Firebase configuration and control notification behavior.

Screen Overview
Reference screenshot for documentation purposes:
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/firebase-configuration/firebase-settings/firebase-settings-bottom.webp
https://userguide.playagegaming.tech/img/cms/security/firebase-configuration/firebase-settings/firebase-settings-top.webp

Toggle Behavior
ON : Firebase integration is enabled.
OFF : Firebase integration is disabled.
After changing any field, click Save Configuration to apply updates.

1) FCM VAPID Configuration
Source:
Firebase Console > Project Settings > Cloud Messaging > Web Push certificates (VAPID key pair).

2) Client-side Configuration
API Key
Auth Domain
Project ID
Storage Bucket
Messaging Sender ID
App ID
Measurement ID
Source:
Firebase Console > Project Settings > General > Your apps > Web app config.

3) Admin SDK Service Account Key
Type
Project ID
Private Key ID
Client Email
Client ID
Token URI
Auth URI
Client X.509 Cert URL
Auth Provider X.509 Cert URL
Universe Domain
Private Key
Source:
Firebase Console > Project Settings > Service accounts > Generate new private key .
Map values from the downloaded JSON file to matching input fields.

Setup Flow
Open your Firebase project in Firebase Console.
Copy the VAPID private key from Cloud Messaging .
Copy web app config values from General > Your apps .
Generate a new service account JSON from Service accounts .
Fill Admin SDK fields using the JSON values.
Set the Firebase toggle to ON .
Click Save Configuration .
Send a test notification to validate the setup.

Update and Maintenance
To update existing settings, edit fields and click Save Configuration again.
To temporarily stop Firebase integration, switch toggle to OFF .
After key rotation (especially private key), update configuration immediately.

Security Notes
Private Key and other credentials are sensitive; limit access to authorized users only.
Never expose these values in public docs or logs.
Incorrect project values can break notification delivery.
